review: {"findings": [{"file_path": "src/revue/core/models.py", "line_number": 41, "severity": "medium", "issue": "AIReview dataclass now carries agent_name as mutable state with empty string default. This creates a silent data-threading risk: findings created without explicit agent_name assignment will have empty strings, making it impossible to distinguish 'agent unknown' from 'agent not set'. This violates the Single Responsibility Principle \u2014 AIReview should represent a code review finding, not track its provenance.", "suggestion": "Consider making agent_name optional (Optional[str] = None) to distinguish unset from empty. Alternatively, move agent_name to a separate FindingMetadata wrapper or context object that travels alongside AIReview but is not part of the core finding contract. This preserves backward compatibility while making the threading explicit.", "confidence": 0.75, "category": "architecture", "agent_name": "leo"}]}

{"findings": [{"file_path": "src/revue/cli.py", "line_number": 383, "severity": "medium", "issue": "Agent display names mapping is incomplete and will silently fail for unknown agents. The _AGENT_DISPLAY_NAMES dict only covers 4 built-in agents, but custom agents or future agents not in this map will return empty string from .get(), leading to labels like ' \u00b7 Code Quality' with a leading space.", "suggestion": "Either validate agent_name against known agents at load time, or handle the empty string case more gracefully. For example: `label = f\"{display_agent} \u00b7 {display_cat}\" if display_agent else display_cat` already handles this, but the leading space issue remains. Consider: `label = f\"{display_agent} \u00b7 {display_cat}\" if display_agent else display_cat` is correct, but document that unknown agents will show category-only labels.", "confidence": 0.72, "category": "code-quality", "agent_name": "maya"}]}

{"findings": [{"file_path": "src/revue/cli.py", "line_number": 617, "severity": "low", "issue": "Code duplication: The agent name + category label formatting logic appears in two places (_format_finding at line 617 and _run_per_issue_dedup at line 889). {"findings": [{"file_path": "src/revue/core/models.py", "line_number": 41, "severity": "low", "issue": "The agent_name field on AIReview is initialized with an empty string default, but there's no validation that it matches a known agent. {"findings": [{"file_path": "src/revue/cli.py", "line_number": 383, "severity": "low", "issue": "The _AGENT_DISPLAY_NAMES mapping is defined at module level but only used in two formatting functions.
